old_value:
{"id":"broadside-3p4v.1","title":"HIGH: (request-target) not required in inbox signature verification","description":"In src/server.rs handle_inbox(), signed_header_names is checked for 'digest' and 'date' (lines 726-733), but '(request-target)' is NOT required. An attacker who obtains a valid signed request for one path (e.g. POST /users/alice/inbox) can replay it against a different path (e.g. POST /users/bob/inbox or POST /inbox). The signature will verify because the (request-target) pseudo-header is not in the signed headers list, so the verification string doesn't include the path.

The server-side verify_signature() function in signatures.rs correctly handles (request-target) when it IS listed in the headers field -- it reconstructs the signed string with the actual request method and path. But because the inbox handler doesn't REQUIRE it, an attacker can craft a Signature header with headers="host date digest" (omitting (request-target)) and the signature will cover only the host, date, and digest -- not the request path. This allows:

1. Cross-inbox replay: A validly-signed request to /users/alice/inbox can be replayed to /users/bob/inbox or /inbox (shared inbox). The actor-mismatch check (line 846) prevents some abuse, but an Undo{Follow} replayed to the shared inbox could unfollow a different persona.

2. Method confusion (theoretical): Without (request-target), the signature doesn't bind the HTTP method. A signed POST could theoretically be replayed as a GET if any GET endpoints accepted Signature headers.

Fix: Add a check requiring '(request-target)' in signed_header_names, alongside the existing digest and date checks. This is a one-line addition.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@reviewcommit.com","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:07:56.00613031Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-20T01:07:56.00613031Z","labels":["security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"broadside-3p4v.1","depends_on_id":"broadside-3p4v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:07:56.029780372Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value:
{"description":"In src/server.rs handle_inbox(), signed_header_names is checked for 'digest' and 'date' (lines 726-733), but neither '(request-target)' nor 'host' is required. An attacker who obtains a valid signed request can replay it against a different path or host.

The server-side verify_signature() function in signatures.rs correctly handles (request-target) when it IS listed in the headers field -- it reconstructs the signed string with the actual request method and path. But because the inbox handler doesn't REQUIRE it, an attacker can craft a Signature header with headers="date digest" (omitting (request-target) and host) and the signature will cover only the date and digest -- not the request path or target host.

Attack scenarios:
1. Cross-inbox replay: A validly-signed request to /users/alice/inbox can be replayed to /users/bob/inbox or /inbox. The actor-mismatch check (line 846) mitigates Follow abuse, but Undo{Follow} replayed to the shared inbox could target a different persona.
2. Cross-host replay: Without 'host' in signed headers, a signature valid for one server can be replayed against another broadside instance (or any server accepting the same signature format).
3. Method confusion (theoretical): Without (request-target), the signature doesn't bind the HTTP method.

Mastodon requires all four: (request-target), host, date, digest.

Fix: Add checks requiring '(request-target)' and 'host' in signed_header_names, alongside the existing digest and date checks (lines 726-733 in server.rs)."}

{"id":"broadside-3p4v.1","title":"HIGH: (request-target) not required in inbox signature verification","description":"In src/server.rs handle_inbox(), signed_header_names is checked for 'digest' and 'date' (lines 726-733), but neither '(request-target)' nor 'host' is required. An attacker who obtains a valid signed request can replay it against a different path or host.

The server-side verify_signature() function in signatures.rs correctly handles (request-target) when it IS listed in the headers field -- it reconstructs the signed string with the actual request method and path. But because the inbox handler doesn't REQUIRE it, an attacker can craft a Signature header with headers="date digest" (omitting (request-target) and host) and the signature will cover only the date and digest -- not the request path or target host.

Attack scenarios:
1. Cross-inbox replay: A validly-signed request to /users/alice/inbox can be replayed to /users/bob/inbox or /inbox. The actor-mismatch check (line 846) mitigates Follow abuse, but Undo{Follow} replayed to the shared inbox could target a different persona.
2. Cross-host replay: Without 'host' in signed headers, a signature valid for one server can be replayed against another broadside instance (or any server accepting the same signature format).
3. Method confusion (theoretical): Without (request-target), the signature doesn't bind the HTTP method.

Mastodon requires all four: (request-target), host, date, digest.

Fix: Add checks requiring '(request-target)' and 'host' in signed_header_names, alongside the existing digest and date checks (lines 726-733 in server.rs).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@reviewcommit.com","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:07:56.00613031Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-20T01:08:54.652409751Z","labels":["security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"broadside-3p4v.1","depends_on_id":"broadside-3p4v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:07:56.029780372Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

new_value: Added (request-target) and host to required signed headers in handle_inbox (server.rs). Now requires all four: (request-target), host, digest, date — matching Mastodon.
